profile.name: Jonisse Pagán
profile.title: Information Systems Technician | CS Student | Programmer

items.detail: Navy veteran and Information Systems Technician (IT rating) with 4 years of active-duty experience maintaining shipboard network infrastructure, cryptographic systems, and voice/data communications. Currently pursuing a B.S. in Computer Science at the University of Puerto Rico, Mayagüez (UPRM), where academic coursework is reinforced by hands-on programming projects in systems design, automation, and web development.
Master of Finance - MIT Sloan (in progress)
B.S. in Computer Science — University of Puerto Rico, Mayagüez (UPRM). Relevant coursework: Databases, Data Structures, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, Software Engineering, Lineal Algebra.

Information Systems Technician (IT) Class A Technical School — Center for Information Warfare Training, Pensacola, FL, 2020. Graduated with honors. Curriculum covered networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP), systems administration, and Navy telecommunications.
High School Diploma — Escuela Superior Eugenio María de Hostos, Mayagüez, PR, 2013.

Information Systems Technician — U.S. Navy, Active Duty, 2019–2023. Administered and maintained LAN/WAN network systems ashore Techical Control Facility - NCTS, supporting 300+ personnel. Managed classified and unclassified computer networks in compliance with STIG and NIST security guidelines.
Operated and maintained Navy Multiband Terminal (NMT) satellite communications systems, ensuring 99.8% uptime during three overseas deployments.
Performed system hardening, vulnerability patching, and quarterly audits on Windows Server and RHEL environments. Trained junior sailors on network troubleshooting procedures and OPSEC policies.
Honorably Discharged, 2023. Final command: NCTS, Virginia.
Programming Languages: Python, C/C++, JavaScript (ES6+), Java, Prolog, HTML/CSS.
Networking & Systems: TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VLANs, Cisco IOS, Windows Server 2019, RHEL 8, Active Directory, VMware.
Cybersecurity: NIST RMF, DISA STIGs, vulnerability scanning (Nessus), basic penetration testing concepts, PKI and cryptographic key management.
Developer Tools: Git, GitHub, VS Code, Linux CLI, Docker (basic).

Personal Web Portfolio (HTML/CSS/JavaScript/Python/Prolog/TypeScript) — Multi-tab portfolio implementing six programming paradigms in the browser: OOP (TypeScript trivia engine), Imperative (Python Black-Scholes pricer via PyScript), Logic (Tau Prolog knowledge base), Functional (JS filter/map pipelines), Asynchronous (async/await JSON fetch), and Event-Driven (DOM event handlers).

Options Flow Scanner (Python) — Command-line tool that parses real-time options tape data to flag unusual institutional activity. Uses volume thresholds, premium filters, and expiration analysis to surface high-conviction trades. Built with pandas and a custom alerting module.

Network Uptime Monitor (Python/Bash) — Automated script that pings a list of hosts, logs response times to a CSV, and emails an alert when a node drops offline. Deployed on a Raspberry Pi during Navy service for personal lab use.

Inventory Management CLI (C++) — Terminal application for tracking hardware assets with CRUD operations, file persistence via binary serialization, and a simple search index. Developed for UPRM Data Structures coursework.
CompTIA Security+ (SY0-701) — Valid 2022–2025. DoD 8570 IAT Level II compliant.
CompTIA Network+ (N10-008) — Issued 2021.
Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) — In progress, expected 2025.
Navy Information Assurance Workforce (IAWF) Certification — IA Technical Level II, 2022.

Navy and Marine Corps Achievement Medal (NAM) — Awarded for superior performance as LAN Administrator during COMPTUEX workup exercise, 2022. Citation recognized zero network downtime across 14-day continuous operations.
Good Conduct Medal — Awarded for four years of honorable, faithful service without disciplinary action, 2023.
UPRM Dean's List — Spring 2024. GPA: 3.7 / 4.0.
Navy Expeditionary Medal · Global War on Terrorism Service Medal · National Defense Service Medal
Spanish — Native proficiency (reading, writing, speaking).
English — Full professional proficiency. All Navy technical training and documentation conducted in English.
UPRM Cinematheqe Student Branch — Active member. Participates in weekly workshops on filming and film festivals.
Coquí Hacks Hackathon (2024) — Competed as part of a 3-person team; developed a real-time campus bus tracker using Python + GTFS data feeds.
Veteran Mentor — Informally guides newly separated veterans transitioning into UPRM, sharing resources for VA education benefits and advising on course sequencing.
